description: Outcome log template must reference ~/work/brain-os-plugin as the source repo, not ~/work/brain
Outcome log must document the action field (not mode field from stash version)
Skill must document the core GH query pattern used to fetch Ready tasks (gh issue list with status:ready label)

Skill must document the simplified claim protocol: atomic status:ready → status:in-progress flip as the lock (replaces prior claim:session- label approach, which broke because gh --add-label won't auto-create labels).

Skill must support /pickup <N> as a direct-claim mode: skip Ready-list filtering, claim the specific issue, load its body (or handover doc), start. So `cr /pickup 100` resolves to issue #100 immediately instead of running the interactive handover-first flow.

Active claim protocol must NOT instruct creating ephemeral claim:session-* labels — they required gh label create before --add-label (failed 'label not found'), didn't actually solve the TOCTOU race, and polluted the label namespace. Historical mention in a race-note explanation is allowed, but no active `gh issue edit --add-label "claim:session-` command should remain.
Skill must have a ## Config block pointing at brain-os.config.md so vault_path + gh_task_repo can be resolved
Skill must document that Seed pages (people, companies, meetings) require owner:human, not owner:bot — seeding needs judgment and auto-promoting them creates false 'in progress' signals
Skill must document that skill creation / modification tasks require owner:human and must start with /grill — skills are design-heavy and auto-executing produces work misaligned with user intent

Owner-label-hygiene § skill creation/modification bullet must route post-/grill creation and behavioral-eval work through /skill-creator (gold standard for variance analysis, triggering accuracy, behavioral evals), distinguishing it from /improve's auto-tuning loop and from coverage-parity hand-edits that mirror new SKILL.md content

/pickup no-args interactive flow must filter the Ready list to owner:human only — /pickup is HITL-only after the #145/#146 split. AFK-eligible (owner:bot) work belongs to /impl auto. Without the filter, the interactive Ready list re-includes bot work and re-creates the surface overlap between /pickup and /impl that the split was meant to eliminate.

Skill must contain a Mid-flow rule section codifying handover/incomplete-grill/mid-draft-writing as owner:human-only. These categories cannot be safely AFK-drained — bots can't resume an interrupted grill or steer a partial draft. The section is the explicit boundary that keeps mid-flow work from being mis-labeled owner:bot and silently auto-promoted by /impl auto.

Mid-flow rule section must enumerate all three categories that stay owner:human only: handovers, incomplete grills, and mid-draft writing. Listing only handovers (the obvious case) leaves the implicit ones — incomplete grills and writing pipelines — unsignposted, which is where the mis-label drift happens in practice.

Skill must document that /pickup auto is deprecated and must NOT spawn workers — instead print a migration message pointing to /impl auto. The flow ID `auto:deprecated` (not `auto`) is the outcome-log marker so /improve can distinguish migration logs from historical auto-mode runs.

Skill must NOT reference scripts/pickup-auto.sh as an active runnable — the script was deleted alongside this refactor (issue #148). Any prose that still tells the LLM to `bash scripts/pickup-auto.sh` would break at runtime with file-not-found and re-create the auto-spawn surface this refactor removed. A historical mention inside the deprecation block is acceptable as long as no active `bash` invocation remains.
